message: Direct fetch() call inside a TanStack Start route loader. Loaders run on the client during navigation; using fetch() directly creates client-side waterfalls and bypasses server-side data fetching. Wrap the call in createServerFn() so it runs on the server with full Node.js access.

Secret environment variable accessed inside a TanStack Start route loader. Route loaders can be compiled into the client bundle, which would expose the secret. Move secret access into a createServerFn() to guarantee server-only execution.

Secret environment variable accessed inside a TanStack Start route loader. Route loaders can be compiled into the client bundle, which would expose the secret. Move secret access into a createServerFn() to guarantee server-only execution.

"use server" is redundant inside a createServerFn().handler() callback. TanStack Start automatically creates a server boundary at the handler boundary — the directive has no effect here and misleads readers. Remove it.

"use server" is redundant inside a createServerFn().handler() callback. TanStack Start automatically creates a server boundary at the handler boundary — the directive has no effect here and misleads readers. Remove it.

useEffect + fetch() used for data fetching in a TanStack Start route component. TanStack Router provides a loader function that runs before the component renders, eliminating this pattern and preventing client-side waterfalls. Move the fetch call to the route's loader and access data via useLoaderData().

useEffect + fetch() used for data fetching in a TanStack Start route component. useEffect + fetch() used for data fetching in a TanStack Start route component. TanStack Router provides a loader function that runs before the component renders, eliminating this pattern and preventing client-side waterfalls. Move the fetch call to the route's loader and access data via useLoaderData().

Calling 'redirect()' or 'notFound()' inside a try-catch block can prevent the navigation from working correctly because TanStack Router uses thrown errors internally to redirect or show a 404 page. Call redirect() outside the try-catch block or rethrow the exception.

Calling 'redirect()' or 'notFound()' inside a try-catch block can prevent the navigation from working correctly because TanStack Router uses thrown errors internally to redirect or show a 404 page. Call redirect() outside the try-catch block or rethrow the exception.

Calling 'redirect()' or 'notFound()' inside a try-catch block can prevent the navigation from working correctly because TanStack Router uses thrown errors internally to redirect or show a 404 page. Call redirect() outside the try-catch block or rethrow the exception.

createServerFn() chain has .handler() before .validator(). The correct order is createServerFn().validator(schema).handler(fn) — calling .handler() first means the validator schema is attached after the handler is already set, so input validation does not run.
